what_it_does: A grounding/confirmation report for the SCENARIO-PLAYER.html mockup: it records 41 candidate UI elements, clustered into 26 dossiers, each carrying an address (ui://...), capabilities, a howto, a maps_to_feature, and a jury verdict, split into 'confirmed' and 'flagged' lists.
observations: Top-level counts: n_candidates 41, n_dossiers 41, n_clusters 26 (note n_dossiers equals n_candidates, not n_clusters).
'ground' block describes the screen as a warm gold-themed control room with personas Vi/Atlas/Nova and phone/desktop frame toggle.

Each dossier has an address using a ui:// scheme (e.g. ui://scenario-player/ptt, ui://chat/reply) and a capabilities list drawn from a small vocabulary: pointable, openable, driven, presentable, spotlit.
Confirmation is decided by a 3-draw jury producing votes_grounded/votes_ungrounded, unanimous, quorum, and an overall verdict.
Two distinct check layers are named: 'refcheck (Layer 1, deterministic floor)' and 'accuracy jury (Layer 2, SOFT — variance not error)'.

Layer 1 floor rejects maps_to_feature values that are neither an inventory id nor 'proposed' — flagged as 'feature fabrication' (e.g. RHM-identity, RHM-thinking, RHM-persona, RHM-listen, RHM-utterance, RHM-status).
Some elements are flagged despite a unanimous grounded jury (e.g. ui://chat/listen, ui://chat/utterance) purely because Layer 1 caught a fabricated feature id.
Two dossiers carry grounding 'built' (ui://chat/toolbar→RUN-run, ui://chat/status→RHM-status); most are grounding 'proposed'.
Two different dossiers share the address ui://chat/utterance with different capabilities and maps_to_feature.
howto.how_to_change frequently names concrete source files (ScenarioPlayer.tsx, RhmChat.tsx, Toolbar.tsx, runtime/suite.py:chat, runtime/suite.py:scenario).
standouts: The two-layer jury: a deterministic floor (refcheck for feature fabrication) plus a soft variance-tolerant accuracy jury, with the floor able to flag even a unanimous-grounded element.
'feature fabrication' as an explicit failure mode: maps_to_feature must resolve to an inventory id or the literal 'proposed'.
Grounded-but-flagged cases reveal the floor and jury are independent gates that both must pass.
conventions: Follows a ui:// addressing scheme and a fixed capability vocabulary.
Each dossier carries a howto triple (what / what_you_can_do / how_to_change) pointing at real surface files.
Jury record shape (verdict, votes_grounded, votes_ungrounded, n, unanimous, quorum) is uniform across all dossiers.
maps_to_feature uses prefixed inventory ids (RHM-*, RUN-*, INB-*, ENG-*) or the sentinel 'proposed'.
concerns: Several maps_to_feature ids the file itself marks as fabricated (RHM-identity, RHM-thinking, RHM-persona, RHM-listen, RHM-utterance, RHM-status) — the file flags these but they remain in the dossiers.
n_dossiers (41) does not match n_clusters (26), so the field naming inside this file is internally ambiguous.
Duplicate address ui://chat/utterance appears in two entries with divergent content.
notes: A QA-style grounding report classifying each Scenario Player UI element as confirmed or flagged via a deterministic floor plus a soft accuracy jury.
questions: What is the authoritative 'inventory' of feature ids that refcheck validates maps_to_feature against?
Why does n_dossiers equal n_candidates (41) rather than n_clusters (26)?
Are 'built' vs 'proposed' grounding values asserted or inferred for ui://chat/toolbar and ui://chat/status?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it presents itself as a grounding/confirmation pass over a mockup's UI elements and the body delivers exactly that with per-element jury verdicts.
apparent_themes: Addressable UI surface (every element gets a ui:// address and capabilities)
Grounding verification with explicit fabrication detection
Two-layer evaluation: deterministic floor + soft probabilistic jury
intention_signals: Auto-generated artefact from a pipeline that proposes UI-element dossiers for a mockup, then gates them through a refcheck floor and a multi-draw accuracy jury, separating safe-to-confirm elements from those needing operator review.
novelty: The 'feature fabrication' floor check that distinguishes inventory-backed feature ids from hallucinated ones, combined with a vote-tally jury that can be overridden by the deterministic layer.
contribution: Contributes a worked instance of element-level UI grounding output for the Scenario Player surface — the address/capability/howto/jury dossier shape and the confirmed/flagged partition with explicit fabrication reasons.
summary_for_embedding: A grounding-confirmation report for the SCENARIO-PLAYER.html mockup listing 41 addressed UI-element dossiers (ui:// addresses, capabilities, howtos, feature mappings) partitioned into confirmed and flagged by a deterministic refcheck floor plus a 3-draw accuracy jury.
intention_for_embedding: To gate auto-proposed UI-element dossiers for a mockup through a fabrication floor and an accuracy jury, surfacing which elements are confirmed and which need operator scrutiny.
